additional_correction.career_03ab1210df1d: removed operational ownership inferred from a sentence that denied productionization ownership

production_delivery_judgments.career_2e516f229493: accepted: explicitly shipped small predictive features
production_delivery_judgments.career_8f5eedb01688: accepted: explicit Docker/Kubernetes deployment of the described SaaS application
production_delivery_judgments.career_9ab513003702: rejected: integration of another team's model-serving service is not delivery ownership

audit_sign_off.tier_a: accepted after full template review and rubric 1.0.3 fixes
audit_sign_off.tier_b: accepted after false-positive review; no direct-requirement leakage found
audit_sign_off.tier_c: accepted after bidirectional review of all six generic-ML templates

base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_00: Applied-ML cohort with strong evaluated-ranking prevalence and mixed production retrieval depth.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_01: Senior explicit-AI cohort with complete retrieval, ranking-evaluation, production, and ownership evidence.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_02: Generic-ML cohort with no career-side ranking evaluation or production retrieval compounds.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_03: Senior explicit-AI cohort with evaluated ranking but only partial production embedding/vector coverage.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_04: Senior explicit-AI cohort with complete production retrieval evidence; LLM context is deep rather than shallow.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_05: JD-anchored plain-language senior system builders; evaluated ranking is universal despite sparse tool keywords.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_06: General-professional catch-all with no direct technical JD evidence.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_07: Data/backend-adjacent cohort with Python and systems evidence but no direct retrieval/ranking compounds.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_08: Senior explicit-AI cohort with complete production retrieval and evaluation evidence.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_09: General-software cohort with product engineering evidence but no direct retrieval/ranking compounds.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_10: Senior explicit-AI cohort with complete production retrieval and evaluation evidence.
base_tier_rationales.fine_atom_11: Single senior explicit-AI profile with complete evidence but maximum small-cohort uncertainty.

interpretation_boundary: This freezes an independent deterministic proxy ranking, not hidden ground truth. The atom-to-tier ordering remains a JD-supported hypothesis and must not be changed after viewing Idea 1 without a new explicitly versioned post-comparison freeze.

worlds.description: Prioritizes audited senior/applied evidence, keeps generic ML borderline, hard-excludes strongest honeypot proxies.
worlds.name: conservative

worlds.description: Gives the plain-language and explicit senior cohorts the strongest Tier-5 prior, matching the JD's senior-system-builder anchor.
worlds.name: senior_heavy

worlds.description: Raises applied-ML candidates when their career templates show ranking/evaluation depth, while keeping senior cohorts high.
worlds.name: applied_ml_friendly

worlds.description: Stress-tests whether generic-ML or data/backend tail exceptions can enter the shortlist when skill overlays are useful.
worlds.name: generic_tail_friendly

worlds.description: Uses the same static structure as the conservative world but gives bounded behavior and availability the largest allowed influence.
worlds.name: behavior_medium

worlds.description: Treats detected honeypot proxies as very strong penalties rather than absolute truth, to measure mutation-policy sensitivity.
worlds.name: mutation_uncertain
